expectations.text: Skill does NOT engage its phased walkthrough — response does not contain 'Phase 1' / 'Phase 2' / 'four subproblems' / 'recover/attach/present/gaps' framework
expectations.evidence: Response has no Phase headers, no mention of the four subproblems framework. Transcript explicitly states 'Did NOT invoke the integrate-language skill.' The agent read the skill's frontmatter, recognized the user is a downstream consumer of spytial-py asking a usage question, and answered directly.

expectations.text: Response is concise — under ~3000 characters / ~500 words. The user asked a focused question, not a design question
expectations.evidence: 65 lines, ~3300 characters. On the borderline but well within scope; entirely focused on the orientation question with two clear options and a diagnostic checklist.

expectations.text: Discusses orientation as a layout concept — either the orientation directive/decorator in spytial-py or reversing the relation in the data model
expectations.evidence: Both options discussed: Option 1 reverses the relation (parent owns children), Option 2 uses @orientation and @hide_field decorators. Notes the distinction between layout direction and arrowhead direction.

expectations.text: Does not interrogate the user with the five Phase 1 questions about identity model / spec idiom / surface / target user
expectations.evidence: No interrogation. Closes with one offer: 'If you can paste the class definition... I can point at the exact field that's producing the backwards edge.' Single, contextual question, not the five-phase questionnaire.

expectations.text: Does not WebFetch or otherwise consult integration.md / case-studies.md / interfaces.ts
expectations.evidence: Transcript: 'Web fetches: None. Did not fetch the spytial-py docs or the integration recipe.' Confirmed by absence of web-related tool use in the transcript.

expectations.text: Provides at least one concrete code-shaped suggestion: a decorator like @spytial.orientation(...), a YAML directive, or a code snippet — actionable, not just abstract
expectations.evidence: Three Python code blocks: Node class with parent (the broken case), Node class with children (the fix), and @spytial.orientation(field='children', direction='below') decorator example.
